rationale: The DAG is unchanged: B1 depends_on [], B2 depends_on [], B3 depends_on [1,2] — B1‖B2→B3 exactly as round 1. Adding cmd/mindspec/config.go to Bead 1 is NOT a new merge-signal problem. It is a 2-bead overlap (B1,B3), below the ≥3 threshold, and it rides the pre-existing, genuinely compile-time B1→B3 edge: because B3 depends on B1, B3 edits config.go atop Bead 1's merged version, so the write is sequential, never parallel. Same shape as the already-blessed architecture.md (B2∩B3) and workflow/interfaces.md (B1∩B3) overlaps. No false dependency is introduced — the edge predates the fix (B3's callers already consume B1's resolvers/PanelGateKeys); the shared file only reinforces an existing real edge and forces no new inter-root edge. Crucially, the two parallel roots B1/B2 stay file-disjoint (config+cmd vs panel), so there is no parallel-write hazard. APPROVE.

findings.item: Merge-signal assessment: cmd/mindspec/config.go now in Bead 1 AND Bead 3
findings.detail: 2-bead overlap (< 3), and the overlap is along the real B1→B3 dependency edge. B3 (depends_on [1,2]) is sequenced after B1, so it edits config.go on top of B1's merged version. No two beads write config.go in parallel; no merge conflict is created. Identical shape to the blessed B2∩B3 architecture.md and B1∩B3 workflow/interfaces.md overlaps.

Frontmatter: id 1 depends_on []; id 2 depends_on []; id 3 depends_on [1,2]. The fix touched only key_file_paths (added cmd/mindspec/config.go + workflow/interfaces.md to Bead 1); no depends_on edge added, removed, or reordered.

The B1→B3 edge already existed in round 1 and is a genuine compile-time dependency (B3's advisory callers + render/JSON functions consume Bead 1's PanelGateAdvisoryDefault / PanelGateExpectedReviewers / exported PanelGateKeys). The shared config.go rides this existing edge rather than manufacturing a new one. No new inter-root (B1–B2) edge is forced.

findings.item: Parallel roots remain file-disjoint (no parallel-write hazard)
findings.detail: B1 files {internal/config/*, cmd/mindspec/config.go, core+workflow interfaces.md} and B2 files {internal/panel/*, ADR-0037, workflow/architecture.md} share zero paths. The plan states this explicitly ('Bead 2 ... touches only architecture.md, so the two root beads stay file-disjoint'). B1 now spanning core+workflow domains is a doc-sync concern (handled by the workflow/interfaces.md ride-along), not a DAG concern.
